channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Ultrafast dynamic stark shift of an exciton-polariton condensate
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68703-x

Ultrafast shaping of exciton-polariton condensates enables applications for classical and quantum logic devices and provides insights into the physics of nonequilibrium quantum condensates in solid-state. Here, the authors demonstrate ultrafast and reversible dynamic Stark modulation of a semiconductor exciton-polariton quantum condensate.
item.dc:title: Ultrafast dynamic stark shift of an exciton-polariton condensate
item.dc:creator: Sarit Feldman
Dmitry Panna
Nadav Landau
Sebastian Brodbeck
Sebastian Klembt
Christian Schneider
Sven Höfling
Alex Hayat

item.title: Ion diffusion overestimates figures of merit in polymeric mixed conductors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 27 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67546-2

There is a challenge of overestimation in figures of merit for organic electrochemical transistors due to a kink in the transistor current. Here, the authors investigate the origin of the kink and identify the charge transport phenomena that is impacted.
item.dc:title: Ion diffusion overestimates figures of merit in polymeric mixed conductors
item.dc:creator: Maryam Shahi
Vianna N. Le
Paula Alarcon Espejo
Bowen Ding
Joel H. Bombile
Chad Risko
Martin Heeney
Alexandra F. Paterson

item.title: Topological robustness of classical and quantum optical skyrmions in atmospheric turbulence
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 27 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68751-3

Optical skyrmions can be generated in free space, but topologically protected information transfer requires skyrmions to be stable upon propagation through complex media. Here, authors demonstrate the topological resilience of classical and quantum optical skyrmions transmitted through experimentally simulated atmospheric turbulence.
item.dc:title: Topological robustness of classical and quantum optical skyrmions in atmospheric turbulence
item.dc:creator: Zhenyu Guo
Cade Peters
Nilo Mata-Cervera
Anton N. Vetlugin
Ruixiang Guo
Pei Zhang
Andrew Forbes
Yijie Shen
